source: City Operations Policy Manual — Foundry IQ Knowledge Base

severity_rules.condition: Active flooding onto road or signs of freezing

resolution_steps: Per Protocol WM-001: Dispatch Water Management emergency crew within 60 minutes.
Isolate the affected pipe section using the zone shut-off valve (ref. Zone Map v4.2).
Erect HSE-approved road barriers and diversion signs per Safety Code SC-14.
Restore water supply to affected residents within SLA window of 4 hours.
File Form INF-7 (Infrastructure Damage Report) and schedule permanent pipe replacement within 14 days.

severity_rules.condition: Pothole >20cm, observed accidents or near-misses, lane blockage
Surface crack, no depth, no incidents

resolution_steps: Per Protocol TR-002: Deploy road maintenance crew within 2 hours for High priority assessments.
Place NHBC-compliant warning cones (minimum 50m ahead) around the hazard zone.
Apply cold-mix asphalt patch immediately for temporary stabilisation per Spec TRS-9.
Schedule permanent hot-mix resurfacing within 48 hours per SLA TR-002.
Conduct post-repair structural inspection of a 50m radius for secondary damage.

severity_rules.condition: Evidence of rodents, pest infestation, or public health risk

resolution_steps: Per Protocol SAN-003: Dispatch sanitation vehicle to the reported location within 4 hours.
Clear all overflowing waste and sanitize the surrounding area with approved disinfectant (Code SAN-R4).
Inspect and tag all damaged or full bin units for replacement under Asset Code BIN-x.
Schedule supplementary collections for the area every 2 days for the following 2 weeks.
File Form SAN-12 and initiate a review of the waste collection frequency schedule for the zone.

severity_rules.condition: 3 or more consecutive lights out, or junction/crossing affected

resolution_steps: Per Protocol ELEC-004: Alert the Electrical Maintenance team (24/7 hotline: EXT-441).
Conduct on-site inspection of the fuse box and circuit breaker for the affected zone (ref. Grid Map v3.1).
Replace faulty LED unit or repair damaged wiring per Electrical Safety Standard ESS-7.
Test all adjacent streetlights on the same circuit for cascading fault detection.
Log the repair in the Asset Management System (AMS) and schedule the next preventive inspection within 6 months.

severity_rules.condition: Disturbance causing public safety concern or repeated complaints

resolution_steps: Per Protocol ENF-005: Dispatch a patrol unit to the location within 1 hour.
Assess the situation and file an Incident Report (Form POL-7).
Issue a verbal or written warning to the offending party as per By-Law NB-3.
Coordinate with the Magistrate's office if violation persists beyond the first warning.
Follow up with the complainant within 24 hours to confirm resolution.
Per Protocol GEN-006: Log the complaint in the City Management System (CMS) within 24 hours.
Assign the ticket to a Field Inspector for in-person assessment.
Inspector to file an assessment report within 48 hours.
Issue a work order based on the inspector's findings.
Update the citizen on the expected resolution timeline via registered contact.
